hook.text: You need shoes. A satisficer buys the first good pair. A maximizer visits 12 stores and still feels unsure.

buildup.text: Herbert Simon coined 'satisficing' in 1956: choosing the first option that meets minimum criteria.

discovery.text: Maximizers compare every option. They often choose better but feel worse. The unchosen options haunt them.

twist.text: More information helps maximizers compare but hurts their satisfaction. Every rejected option creates regret.

climax.text: Design for satisficers: show one clear recommendation, highlight one best option, and reduce comparison urge.

punchline.text: Good enough is often best. Design for satisficers, not exhaustive comparison.

quiz.question: Why are maximizers often less satisfied than satisficers?
options: Every unchosen option creates regret — maximizers compare endlessly and the alternatives haunt them
Satisficers have lower standards and are easier to please
